verses.1: “Behold, my eye has seen all this, My ear has heard and understood it.
verses.2: “What you know I also know; I am not inferior to you.
verses.3: “But I would speak to the Almighty, And I desire to argue with God.
verses.4: “But you smear me with lies; You are all worthless physicians.
verses.5: “Oh that you would be completely silent, And that it would become your wisdom!
verses.6: “Please hear my argument, And give your attention to the contentions of my lips.
verses.7: “Will you speak what is unjust for God, And speak what is deceitful for Him?
verses.8: “Will you show partiality for Him? Will you contend for God?
verses.9: “ Will it go well when He examines you? Or will you deceive Him as one deceives a man?
verses.10: “He will certainly punish you If you secretly show partiality.
verses.11: “Will His majesty not terrify you, And the dread of Him fall upon you?
verses.12: “Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes, Your defenses are defenses of clay.
verses.13: “Be silent before me so that I may speak; Then let come upon me what may.
verses.14: “Why should I take my flesh in my teeth, And put my life in my hands?
verses.15: “Though He slay me, I will hope in Him. Nevertheless I will argue my ways before Him.
verses.16: “This also will be my salvation, For a godless person cannot come before His presence.
verses.17: “Listen carefully to my speech, And let my declaration fill your ears.
verses.18: “Behold now, I have prepared my case; I know that I will be vindicated.
verses.19: “Who could contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
verses.20: “Only two things I ask that You do not do to me, Then I will not hide from Your face:
verses.21: Remove Your hand from me, And may the dread of You not terrify me.
verses.22: “Then call and I will answer; Or let me speak, then reply to me.
verses.23: “How many are my guilty deeds and sins? Make known to me my wrongdoing and my sin.
verses.24: “Why do You hide Your face And consider me Your enemy?
verses.25: “Will You scare away a scattered leaf? Or will You pursue the dry chaff?
verses.26: “For You write bitter things against me And make me inherit the guilty deeds of my youth.
verses.27: “You put my feet in the stocks And watch all my paths; You set a limit for the soles of my feet,
verses.28: While I am decaying like a rotten thing, Like a garment that is moth-eaten.
